cards.front: CCNA 200-301 total exam time?
cards.back: 120 minutes

cards.front: 120 minutes
cards.back: CCNA 200-301 total exam time?

cards.front: CCNA 200-301 question count?
cards.back: Approximately 100 questions (varies 90-110)

cards.front: Approximately 100 questions (varies 90-110)
cards.back: CCNA 200-301 question count?

cards.front: Roughly how many sim/lab questions on CCNA 200-301?
cards.back: 4 to 6 (lab simulations + drag-drop + sim mini-labs)

cards.front: 4 to 6 (lab simulations + drag-drop + sim mini-labs)
cards.back: Roughly how many sim/lab questions on CCNA 200-301?

cards.front: Approximate weight of sim/lab questions on CCNA?
cards.back: ~30% of total score — each sim worth much more than one MCQ

cards.front: ~30% of total score — each sim worth much more than one MCQ
cards.back: Approximate weight of sim/lab questions on CCNA?

cards.front: Can you navigate backward to a previous question on CCNA?
cards.back: No. Once submitted, locked. Plan time forward only.

cards.front: No. Once submitted, locked. Plan time forward only.
cards.back: Can you navigate backward to a previous question on CCNA?

cards.front: Time budget per multi-choice question?
cards.back: About 45-60 seconds. Faster on easy ones to bank time for sims.

cards.front: About 45-60 seconds. Faster on easy ones to bank time for sims.
cards.back: Time budget per multi-choice question?

cards.front: Time budget per sim/lab?
cards.back: 10-15 minutes max. Hard cap. Move on at 15.

cards.front: 10-15 minutes max. Hard cap. Move on at 15.
cards.back: Time budget per sim/lab?

cards.front: Why is hard-capping sim time critical?
cards.back: No back-navigation. Spending 25 min on one sim = losing 30 MCQs you never reach. Even partial credit on a sim beats 30 unanswered questions.

cards.front: First action when a sim opens (before reading prompt)?
cards.back:
1. Note the time remaining
2. Scan the topology diagram
3. Click into each device once to verify console works
4. THEN read the prompt

cards.front: Why scan topology before reading prompt?
cards.back: Prompt references "the LAN" / "R2" / "the link" — diagram is the noun dictionary. Pre-loading topology = faster prompt parsing.

cards.front: If sim has multiple sub-questions, what do you do first?
cards.back: Read ALL sub-questions before configuring anything. Later parts may constrain earlier config choices.

cards.front: Read ALL sub-questions before configuring anything. Later parts may constrain earlier config choices.
cards.back: If sim has multiple sub-questions, what do you do first?

cards.front: Drill — sub-question pre-read prevents what failure?
cards.back: Configuring sub-Q1 in a way that breaks the requirement of sub-Q3. You undo work because you didn't see ahead.

cards.front: Configuring sub-Q1 in a way that breaks the requirement of sub-Q3. You undo work because you didn't see ahead.
cards.back: Drill — sub-question pre-read prevents what failure?

cards.front: When stuck on a sim at the 10-min mark, what's the strategy?
cards.back: Switch to partial-credit mode. Configure what you DO know (interface IPs, hostnames, anything baseline). Partial > zero.

cards.front: Switch to partial-credit mode. Configure what you DO know (interface IPs, hostnames, anything baseline). Partial > zero.
cards.back: When stuck on a sim at the 10-min mark, what's the strategy?

cards.front: When stuck at 15 minutes — action?
cards.back: Submit what you have, move on. Sunk-cost trap kills the rest of the exam.

cards.front: Submit what you have, move on. Sunk-cost trap kills the rest of the exam.
cards.back: When stuck at 15 minutes — action?

cards.front: Why is `show running-config` your first sim command?
cards.back: Sims often have partial pre-loaded config. Don't assume blank. Read what's there first — Q may want you to ADD, not replace.

cards.front: Why verify with `show` after every config block (in a sim)?
cards.back: Catches typos and missing pieces while you can still fix. Hitting submit on broken config = zero on that sub-question.

cards.front: Catches typos and missing pieces while you can still fix. Hitting submit on broken config = zero on that sub-question.
cards.back: Why verify with `show` after every config block (in a sim)?

cards.front: What do you do with extra time after finishing all questions?
cards.back: Nothing — can't go back. Make sure you submitted, then breathe. No review pass exists on CCNA.

cards.front: Nothing — can't go back. Make sure you submitted, then breathe. No review pass exists on CCNA.
cards.back: What do you do with extra time after finishing all questions?

cards.front: Mental model: which exam section eats most time?
cards.back: Sim/lab — 4-6 sims × 15 min cap = 60-90 min of 120-min budget. MCQs get the leftover.

cards.front: Sim/lab — 4-6 sims × 15 min cap = 60-90 min of 120-min budget. MCQs get the leftover.
cards.back: Mental model: which exam section eats most time?

cards.front: Working backward from 120 min — how much for MCQs if 6 sims at 12 min each?
cards.back: 120 - (6 × 12) = 48 min for ~95 MCQs = ~30 sec each. Tight. Move fast on familiar MCQs.

cards.front: 120 - (6 × 12) = 48 min for ~95 MCQs = ~30 sec each. Tight. Move fast on familiar MCQs.
cards.back: Working backward from 120 min — how much for MCQs if 6 sims at 12 min each?

cards.front: When is it OK to skip a sim entirely and take the zero?
cards.back: Almost never — partial credit possible on most. Only if you have <2 min left and 10 unanswered MCQs remain. MCQ guesses have 25% expected value.

cards.front: Almost never — partial credit possible on most. Only if you have <2 min left and 10 unanswered MCQs remain. MCQ guesses have 25% expected value.
cards.back: When is it OK to skip a sim entirely and take the zero?

cards.front: Pre-exam drill — what should you have memorized cold to save sim time?
cards.back:
- ACL syntax (extended + standard)
- OSPF basic config (`router ospf 1` / `network` / `passive-interface default`)
- Static route syntax with AD
- VLAN + trunk config
- `show ip route`, `show ip ospf neighbor`, `show running-config interface`

cards.front: What does muscle-memory of `do show` save you in a sim?
cards.back: Don't have to `end` then `show` then re-enter config. `do show ip route` from inside `config-if` keeps you in context — saves seconds × every sim.

cards.front: Calculator/notepad availability on CCNA exam?
cards.back: Yes — on-screen scratch pad provided. Use for subnetting math and tracking sub-question constraints.

cards.front: Yes — on-screen scratch pad provided. Use for subnetting math and tracking sub-question constraints.
cards.back: Calculator/notepad availability on CCNA exam?

cards.front: What should the scratch pad track during a sim?
cards.back:
1. Numbered requirements from prompt
2. Subnet math (host counts, mask boundaries)
3. ACL line plan before typing
4. Tick boxes as you complete each requirement

cards.front: Anti-pattern: typing config before understanding the prompt — why does this cost time?
cards.back: Wrong config requires undo (`no` form of every line) + retype. Net loss vs spending 2 min reading first.

cards.front: Final 5 minutes of exam — strategy if questions remain?
cards.back: Guess on remaining MCQs (25% > 0%). Submit any partial sim work. Never leave blanks — CCNA has no negative marking.

cards.front: Guess on remaining MCQs (25% > 0%). Submit any partial sim work. Never leave blanks — CCNA has no negative marking.
cards.back: Final 5 minutes of exam — strategy if questions remain?

cards.front: CCNA negative marking?
cards.back: No. Wrong answer = 0 points. Blank answer = 0 points. Always guess.

cards.front: No. Wrong answer = 0 points. Blank answer = 0 points. Always guess.
cards.back: CCNA negative marking?
